item.title: Pokopia Player Builds Church of Ditto
item.description:
A creative Pokemon Pokopia player has constructed a stunning church dedicated to the game's star Pokemon, Ditto. The humorous Pokopia build includes pews, stained glass windows, and more.

The sandbox spin-off of the massively popular monster-catching franchise has been a smash hit on the Nintendo Switch 2 since its release. Pokopia quickly became one of the fastest-selling games on Nintendo's newest home console, moving 2.2 million units worldwide within just four days of its release. Fans have also quickly taken to the wide array of options for building their dream homes in the game, using the game's construction systems to create impressive Pokopia tributes to real-world locations like Taco Bell and iconic Pokemon settings. Now, one Pokopia player has used the game to build a shrine to its star Pokemon, Ditto.

Pokopia Player Builds Incredible Church Devoted to Ditto

Reddit user JonesySteelblade shared their Church of Ditto on the Pokopia subreddit, featuring multiple images of the build. The showcase begins with an outside view of the church, built along the main road in Rocky Ridges, with steeple roofs and stained-glass windows. The interior uses Pokopia 's park benches to craft multiple rows of pews, with a carpeted walkway leading up to a raised altar at the rear of the church. The ceremonial bread and wine are also represented on a side table in the church using some of Pokopia 's food items.

The impressive church build in Pokopia drew plenty of jokes from fellow fans in the post's comments. One fan joked that the Pokemon villagers in Pokopia had "found religion" only a month after the game's release, with others creating religious slogans for the church. The Pokopia project was clearly a hit with fans across the subreddit as well, amassing nearly 10,000 upvotes.

Pokopia has seen a multitude of creative fan projects since its debut in early March, recreating real-world restaurants, functional facilities, and much more. Fans have looked to provide modern upgrades to their Pokemon villagers' housing, including one who created a full apartment building in Pokopia . Real-world restaurants have been a popular topic for many fans looking to flex their creative muscles, including re-imagined versions of chains like Olive Garden and Taco Bell. Some players have even pushed the game a step further, undertaking massive projects like removing the ocean from their in-game world.

New Questions Added!

GameRant Quiz

—

QUESTION 1/1

Results

0

—

More Games
New Questions Added!
GameRant Quiz
Easy (15s)Medium (10s)Hard (5s)Permadeath (5s)
—
QUESTION 1/1
Results
0
—
More Games

Pokopia has given fans plenty to do beyond its building systems, with developers Omega Force and Game Freak recently confirming its second official event. Omega Force and Game Freak revealed a brand-new event running in mid-April, with Bulbasaur's Jump Rope Contest scheduled to start on April 19. Players using time travel in Pokopia have confirmed that rewards like Lost Relics, Silver and Rainbow Feathers, and even Pokemetal will be available for achieving high scores in the event. Pokopia 's next event looks to provide even more to keep fans coming back to the successful spin-off.

9 /10

Pokemon Pokopia

item.title: The Elder Scrolls 6 Exclusivity Leaves Gamers Frustrated
item.description:
This article was updated by Andrea Trama on April 18, 2026, with reference to recent rumors about Microsoft rethinking its exclusivity for games. It was first published on April 10.

When Microsoft acquired Elder Scrolls 6 developer Bethesda back in 2021, it was naturally assumed that all of its upcoming projects — including the long-anticipated entry in its iconic fantasy franchise — would be Xbox console exclusives. Documents from the Microsoft-FTC suggested as much, too. However, as time has gone on, this feels a bit less likely. Not only has Xbox kept some truly massive titles like Call of Duty multiplatform, but first-party games are coming to PlayStation left and right. While this started as games that “made sense” on PlayStation, like Hellblade 2 , Indiana Jones , Hi-Fi Rush , and so on, hell has since frozen over and the most iconic Xbox games will soon be playable on the competition’s hardware.

While Gears of War and Halo coming to PlayStation essentially signals that the console war is over, which could have been enough to have players assuming The Elder Scrolls 6 would be on Sony’s consoles, the latest Xbox port basically seals the deal. After spending a good bit of time restricted to Xbox and PC, the 2023 Bethesda RPG Starfield is now on PS5, which opens the floodgates for an Elder Scrolls 6 PS5 release. Full exclusivity may have been the plan at some point, but with Xbox fully embracing Game Pass and a multiplatform approach, that plan is sure to change. Unfortunately, though, this remains a topic of speculation instead of something concrete to go off of, leaving players frustrated that they’re still in the dark.

The Lack of Answers About Elder Scrolls 6’s Exclusivity is Needlessly Frustrating

The 11-month-old thread above remains relevant, because at nearly the one-year mark, Bethesda fans are still stuck asking the exact same questions. Every few months, a similar thread is posted where curious players wonder whether they’ll need an Xbox or PC to play Elder Scrolls 6 , and every time the same kind of responses are provided. Users like Weeaboo20909 will talk about how they “doubt it,” as Elder Scrolls 6 being so likely to launch with Xbox Game Pass would mean Microsoft is losing out on a massive amount of revenue it could be receiving by selling full-priced versions on PlayStation. Apart from “cutting out a market,” users such as SiegeRewards pointed to the multiplatform launch for Oblivion Remastered as further proof of Microsoft’s plans for the series’ releases.

According to insider Jez Corden, Xbox is having major discussions about exclusivity, though it's unclear if this is currently leaning one way or the other. If true, it does explain why the company has yet to commit to one answer regarding The Elder Scrolls 6's exclusivity.

However, it is still possible that The Elder Scrolls 6 has some form of exclusivity to make the Xbox version more appealing to potential buyers. Perhaps it’ll get some exclusive in-game bonuses like armor sets, but beyond that, Big_AngeBosstecoglu’s theory about it being a “timed exclusive” like Indiana Jones would make perfect sense. A wait as long as Starfield is improbable given that it’s an outlier, as that game launched before Xbox had started fully embracing PlayStation ports. Instead, a six-month wait feels the most realistic if The Elder Scrolls 6 does not release everywhere at once. But, again, this is just theorizing from fans, as everyone is still being left in the dark and there's still no release date in sight.

The frustration comes from plenty of different directions. There are some Xbox fans who are naturally disappointed in the multiplatform approach, as a big exclusive like Elder Scrolls 6 would encourage gamers to buy an Xbox, keeping things competitive and pushing PlayStation to try its best to keep up. Microsoft’s acquisition of huge companies like Bethesda and Activision, yet a lack of using them to strengthen the brand, remains disappointing to those who want to see Xbox thrive. Another issue with the lack of transparency is that gamers should be able to properly plan for the game. If PlayStation fans know they’ll need to get a PC or Xbox, then they could use the extra heads up to begin saving for it. And if they’re told it will eventually come to PlayStation after a timed exclusivity period, then they can decide whether they want to wait. TES 6 is likely the second most anticipated game in the world behind GTA 6 , so it’s puzzling how little is known about it after so long.

It May Be a While Before Elder Scrolls 6 Fans Get Exclusivity Answers

Players still asking the exact same questions that they were almost a year ago is reflective of the general annoyance in the wait for Elder Scrolls 6 . Revealed all the way back in 2018, there’s a very real possibility that there’s a 10-year gap between this unveiling and the game’s launch. Todd Howard’s recent comments about the early reveal being a mistake and lighthearted suggestion that fans pretend it didn’t happen do little to dissuade concerns that the game is still years away. And if that’s the case, then there’s a danger of The Elder Scrolls 6 exclusivity question continuing to be asked ad nauseam. Even if players are still months away from seeing the game in action, it would be great to see Howard or another member of Bethesda shed some light on whether the game will or will not be Xbox exclusive. Doing so wouldn’t require any kind of flashy reveal, but would create some much-needed, refreshing discussion.

5 Questions
Will Elder Scrolls 6 Stay Xbox-Only? Test Your Knowledge on This Lingering Question
Your Top Score

item.title: Cast Your Votes to Decide the Top 100 Best Games of All Time
item.description:
This article was updated by Andrea Trama on April 18, 2026, to reflect the change from top 10 to top 100 games. It was first published on April 15.

GameRant readers have the opportunity to let their voices be heard and help determine the 100 games that deserve to be called the very best the industry has ever seen. Voting is simple. Two critically-acclaimed games are pitted against each other, and you pick the one that you like more. You can vote as many times as you want, with the list updated regularly as more votes are counted.

The initial batch of nearly 80,000 votes left us with a 10 Best Games of All Time list that was led by Rockstar's open world western Red Dead Redemption 2 , followed by The Elder Scrolls 5: Skyrim , Elden Ring , The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt , Batman: Arkham City , Resident Evil 4 , God of War (2018) , Metal Gear Solid , Cyberpunk 2077 , and God of War Ragnarok . Since then, hundreds of thousands more votes have been cast, shaking up the top 10 quite a bit, and forming a clearer top 100 list now.

Vote for the Best Games of All Time

If you're curious to see how the list of the 100 Best Video Games Ever Made has changed, you can see the current top 10 by clicking "See Results." The list will continue to evolve as more votes are cast, and it will be very interesting to see which games fall off and which games manage to take one of the coveted spots for themselves.

While GameRant is doing a top 100 list, the widget only shows the top 10.

Vote for your favorite games to help determine the titles that are truly the best of the best. The poll never closes, so you can vote as much as you'd like to try to secure your favorite games' spots in the top 100.

item.title: Is The Switch 2 Worth It Now?
item.description:
A little over a year ago, Nintendo unveiled the Nintendo Switch 2 to the world, officially joining the current console generation while also creating a direct successor to the company's highest-selling system, the Nintendo Switch. When it finally came out, this hybrid system got a mixed reception from some critics, but it managed to sell over 17 million units in less than a year.

As the Nintendo Switch 2’s first anniversary is getting closer and closer, we must ask: Is the console worth buying now? After all, purchasing an all-new video game system is quite a compromise, mainly due to how pricey they are, so it's crucial to know all of their pros and cons before making a decision. Truth be told, however, the Switch 2 is completely worth it right now for various good reasons, and this article will answer every major question someone who’s still hesitant about it may have.

Upcoming Releases

Is The Switch 2 Worth The Money?

Yes, Especially When Compared To The Competition

Back when Nintendo revealed the Switch 2, it was heavily criticized for its initial price of $450 , meaning that it was $150 more expensive than its predecessor’s launch price and cost $50 less than a PlayStation 5, despite being significantly less powerful. However, Nintendo ended up having the last laugh, as its two competitors, the PS5 and the Xbox Series X/S, ended up getting egregious price increases shortly after the Switch 2 came out, with the hybrid system currently being the cheapest option of the trio.

Additionally, the Switch 2 not only already has a great collection of exclusives, but it’s also perfect for anybody who may have skipped the last console generation and never owned a Switch 1, seeing as it supports backwards compatibility with that system. Nintendo’s last console had a lifespan of over 8 years (and counting), and it’s safe to say that the same will happen to its successor, so it’s definitely a worthy investment.

What Are The Must-Play Switch 2 Games?

Donkey Kong Bananza, Mario Kart World, Pokemon Pokopia, And Many More

Nintendo knows more than any other console manufacturer on the market that the best way to entice people into buying its hardware is to offer a collection of high-quality exclusive software, and the Switch 2 is obviously no exception to this rule. For starters, the console’s signature launch title was Mario Kart World , the next entry in the popular kart-racing series that spiced things up with an ambitious and rewarding open-world mode.

Many other great Switch 2 exclusives have come out in the last few months, with Pokemon Pokopia being a cozy life simulator that combines the best aspects of the Pokemon and Animal Crossing franchises, Hyrule Warriors: Age of Imprisonment being a fast-paced Musou game with a compelling narrative set in the Legend of Zelda universe, and Donkey Kong Bananza being such a groundbreaking and addictive 3D platformer that it was even nominated for GOTY at The Game Awards 2025. The Switch 2 truly has something for everyone, and it’s worth purchasing one just to experience these modern masterpieces.

Are There Any Good Exclusives Coming Soon?

Yes: The Duskbloods, Fire Emblem: Fortune's Weave, And Pokemon Winds And Waves Are Some Of The Biggest Ones

The Nintendo Switch 2 has had a very solid first year (which technically isn’t over yet), and yet, many people believe that its lineup of exclusives left a lot to be desired. Whether you agree with that sentiment or not, it’s impossible to deny that this console’s catalog will get even more alluring in the next few months.

2026 will see the release of Fire Emblem: Fortune’s Weave , the next mainline entry in Nintendo’s iconic strategy RPG series, The Duskbloods , FromSoftware’s Switch 2-exclusive multiplayer “Soulslike”, and Splatoon Raiders , an intriguing spin-off set in the Splatoon universe, not to mention that the 10 th Generation of Pokemon will arrive to the console in 2027, with the upcoming Pokemon Winds and Waves . On top of all that, there are currently unverified rumors floating around claiming Nintendo is working on a Switch 2 remake of Ocarina of Time and a brand-new Star Fox title, so there might be even more great games on the way that the company has yet to announce in an official capacity.

Does It Have Third-Party Support?

Yes, Even More So Than Previous Nintendo Systems

Previous Nintendo consoles were criticized for their lack of third-party support (often caused by their outdated graphical capabilities and unconventional controllers), with the Wii and the Wii U being the most infamous offenders. Fortunately, however, the Switch 1 did get a lot more ports of third-party games, which was a step in the right direction, but they were often compromised in one way or another, like being turned into cloud versions or requiring a hefty internet download despite being sold in a cartridge.

Nintendo was very vocal about how much third-party support the Switch 2 was going to get from the moment they revealed the console, seeing as beloved games like Cyberpunk 2077: Ultimate Edition , Hogwarts Legacy , and Street Fighter 6 were launch titles with very minimal graphical downgrades. To make things even better, various other companies have been releasing their games on this hybrid console throughout the last few months, and they all play surprisingly well, with some of the most notorious examples including Resident Evil Requiem and Final Fantasy 7 Remake . And with ports of Indiana Jones and the Great Circle , 007 First Light , and Final Fantasy 7 Rebirth on the horizon, it's safe to say that the Switch 2's third-party support is getting even better.

Aren’t Most Of Its Games Available On Switch 1?

Not Really, That Only Applies To Cross-Generational Titles That Were Announced Before The Switch 2's Release

A common misconception regarding the Switch 2 is the belief that most of its exclusives are cross-generational, meaning there's no reason to buy one when somebody already owns the original Switch. In reality, this cross-generational nature only applies to games that were announced way before the Switch 2’s reveal, like Pokemon Legends: Z-A and Metroid Prime 4: Beyond , as it just makes sense to make them available on both consoles, with one version looking and running slightly better.

It’s also worth pointing out that multiple Switch 1 games have gotten Switch 2 editions that enhanced their visuals, with most of them even adding brand-new content that would have looked a lot worse in the last-gen console, like Super Mario Bros. Wonder + Meetup in Bellabel Park and Kirby and the Forgotten Land + Star-Crossed World . The Switch 2 is not only backwards compatible with Switch 1 games, but it’s also capable of improving them both in terms of graphics and gameplay.

What Can The Switch 2 Do That The Other Consoles Can’t?

It’s A Hybrid Console, Plus It Has A Few Unique Gimmicks That Help It Stand Out

There’s no denying that, among all the systems of the home console market’s "Big 3," the Nintendo Switch 2 is the most distinct one. Obviously enough, it’s a hybrid device (just like its predecessor), meaning that it can either be played on a TV or carried around anywhere like a handheld without the need for any additional apps or peripherals.

Nintendo always likes to play around with its consoles and add a bunch of innovative features that could lead to unique gameplay experiences. The Switch 2 Joy-Cons are not only way more responsive than their Switch 1 counterparts, but they also have a brand-new mouse mode that’s perfect for browsing the eShop and playing a select number of games that may require more precision. On top of that, there’s also Nintendo Switch Online: Nintendo Classics , a subscription service that gives players access to thousands of retro games from older consoles like the NES, the SNES, the GBA, the Sega Genesis, the N64, and the GameCube, with the last one’s digital catalog being 100% exclusive to this system.

Why Shouldn’t I Wait For A More Powerful Re-Edition? (Like The PS5 Pro)

Nintendo Doesn’t Usually Release Enhanced Versions Mid-Generation

Every time a new home console comes out, many people think that they can just skip it and wait a few years until its mid-gen enhanced version comes out. Of course, Sony is often guilty of doing this, as the PS5 got a re-edition in 2025, known as the PS5 Pro , whose mere existence people had predicted since the console’s original launch back in November 2020.

Subscribe to our newsletter for Switch 2 buying clarity

Still undecided? Subscribe to the newsletter for in-depth Switch 2 analysis, practical buying guidance, curated game spotlights, and thoughtful perspectives to help you make a confident purchase decision.
By subscribing, you agree to receive newsletter and marketing emails, and accept our Terms of Use and Privacy Policy. You can unsubscribe anytime.

Nevertheless, it’s not recommended to wait for a hypothetical Switch 2 Pro because the Big N doesn’t usually make mid-generation re-editions. Yes, the Switch 1 did technically get two, but neither of them focused on making the console more powerful, as the Switch OLED has a better screen, and the Switch Lite is handheld-only and can’t be plugged into a TV. Anyone who is even mildly interested in getting a Switch 2 should buy one right now, as it’s unlikely to get an enhanced re-edition, not to mention that the base version is already quite powerful for a hybrid system of this nature.

Will It Be More Expensive Later?

Most Likely, Especially Since The PS5 And The Xbox Series X/S Already Had Price Increases

Let’s face it: the medium of gaming is getting more and more expensive every year. The prices of both the PS5 and the Xbox Series X have increased on more than one occasion since their initial release, and they cost $650 right now. To make things even worse, analysts predict that their next-gen successors, the PS6 and Project Helix, could end up being priced at up to $1000 if hardware manufacturing costs don’t go down before the 2030s.

Nintendo doesn’t usually increase the prices of its systems in the middle of a generation. In fact, this company prefers to just never reduce them or to release cheaper, less powerful alternatives like the Switch Lite. While it’s highly unlikely for the Switch 2 to get a price increase anytime soon, things are still quite uncertain, unfortunately. Anybody who is still interested in getting one (and can afford it) should stop hesitating and just do it as soon as possible, because absolutely nobody knows if the Switch 2 will get more expensive a year or so from now.

item.title: Best Meta 1911 Build in Black Ops 7 & Warzone
item.description:
Call of Duty: Black Ops 7’s Season 3 has reached its third week, and with it comes a new set of Weekly Challenges. By completing them, players can unlock the 1911, the iconic pistol that many Zombies fans will enjoy using, as it brings back the Mustang and Sally. However, for Multiplayer and Warzone players, it also serves as a solid secondary weapon. Here is the best meta build for the 1911 in both Black Ops 7 and Warzone .

Best Meta 1911 Build for Black Ops 7 Multiplayer

Best Attachments:

  • P-45 Strike Comp (Muzzle)
  • 6.4'' Long Slide Barrel (Barrel)
  • Sirius Extended Mag (Magazine)
  • Eaves Quick Grip (Rear Grip)
  • Sear Fast Trigger (Fire Mods)

The 1911 is definitely one of the top pistols in its category, offering solid stopping power. With the build above, players get a weapon with a reliable two-shot kill to the head and upper torso at 10 meters , while maintaining a consistent two-shot headshot kill at 25 meters. However, this drops to a three-shot headshot at 50 meters.

With the attachments listed above, the 1911 has relatively low recoil and gun kick , an incredibly fast draw speed, along with an improved fire rate and increased damage range for a quick time to kill. However, this build does not make use of the weapon’s Prestige Attachment, which turns it fully automatic, as the recoil becomes quite difficult to manage if you choose to use it.

Best Meta 1911 Build for Warzone

Best Attachments:

  • P-45 Strike Comp (Muzzle)
  • 6.4'' Long Slide Barrel (Barrel)
  • Rigel Extended II (Magazine)
  • Eaves Quick Grip (Rear Grip)
  • Sear Fast Trigger (Fire Mods)

Our next build for Warzone is almost identical to the previous, only this time making use of the Rigel Extended II for an extra five bullets per magazine. While this might not seem like much, since players have more health in Warzone , those extra shots go a long way. Just like in Multiplayer, this build delivers excellent damage, offering a reliable four-shot kill to the head up to 25 meters , but requiring an extra shot at 50 meters.

While this build does take a small hit to aim-down-sight and sprint-to-fire speed, it is still notably fast. Recoil is relatively easy to control as well, though slightly more noticeable than its Multiplayer counterpart. Where this build really shines is within the 10-meter range , making it an excellent backup option to pair with a long-range Assault Rifle or Light Machine Gun.

  • Call of Duty: Black Ops 7
    Released
    November 14, 2025

    WHERE TO PLAY
